output_debit_classifier.reason: When a corporation's board declares a cash dividend, the declaration creates a legal obligation to pay shareholders. At the declaration date, the company records a liability (Dividends Payable) and reduces retained earnings. The actual cash payment will be recorded separately when the dividend is distributed in 30 days. This transaction affects only equity and liabilities; no assets are affected until payment date.
